### Sr. GenAI ML Data Scientist Prompt Engineer at [Microchip Technology Inc.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/microchip-technology) (Current)
...
Leading enterprise Generative AI initiatives at Microchip Technology focused on semiconductor knowledge intelligence, yield optimization, and secure large language model deployment across engineering and manufacturing ecosystems. Architected multi-stage RAG platforms leveraging GPT-4, LangChain, and Pinecone to enable context-aware retrieval over decades of IP and fab process documentation. Developed domain-adapted LLaMA-3 models using LoRA fine-tuning, built scalable Spark/Airflow data pipelines, and implemented structured entity extraction using transformer-based NLP models. Established production-grade MLOps with MLflow, Docker, and Kubernetes, along with real-time monitoring and drift detection. Designed secure hybrid cloud architectures with encryption, IAM, and tokenization controls to protect sensitive semiconductor IP while enabling scalable AI-driven decision support.

results.title: Furkhan Suhail
highlights:
Data Science Engineer at Microchip Technology Inc.
...
Summary: Results-driven Data Engineer with 5 years of experience leveraging machine learning, deep learning, and statistical analysis to drive data-driven decision-making. Proficient in Python, SQL, with a strong background in NLP, regression and predictive analytics. Passionate about transforming raw data into actionable insights and building scalable AI solutions. • Programming Languages and Database Management: Python, Embedded C, C++, SQL (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Snowflake) • Machine Learning/Deep Learning: scikit-learn, TensorFlow, XGBoost, Random Forest, Keras, PyTorch, Sentiment Analysis, NLTK, Gensim, NLP • Transformer Models: BERT, LSTM, Large Language Models (LLMs), OpenAI, LLaMA, Hugging Face • Data Engineering & Analytics: ETL Pipelines, Pandas, PySpark, Data Mining, Data Wrangling • Data Visualization and GUI: Tableau, Power BI, Seaborn, Matplotlib, Tkinter • Software Development & Version Control: Agile, Scrum, DevOps, Object-Oriented Programming (OOP), Confluence, Git • Data Science and AI Applications: Artificial Intelligence, Deep Learning, Generative AI, Machine Learning Algorithms, Neural Networks • Cloud Deployment and Scheduling: AWS (EC2, Lambda, SageMaker, Bedrock), Kubernetes, CI/CD, Apache Kafka, MCP Protocol
...
### Data Scientist at [Microchip Technology Inc.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/microchip-technology)
...
As a Support Engagement Engineer (SEE), I analyze customer engagement data and generate reports to identify trends in customer expectations, concerns, needs, and growth opportunities when using Microchip products and services. Leveraging my expertise in embedded systems and data science, I identify and monitor key factors influencing customer behavior and feedback. The role is research-intensive, requiring me to stay updated on common client equipment, understand their technological challenges, and provide innovative guidance to help clients adopt the latest industry trends. Additionally, the position demands proficiency in application development using Python and C, data science, and data analytics. I summarize client needs to deliver quality control feedback to Microchip’s technical support team and establish a standardized engagement approach to empower clients, encourage self-sufficiency, and promote system-level solutions. In essence, my role involves summarizing client needs to deliver quality control feedback to Microchip Technologies support team using data science and data analytics on KPI (Key Performance Indicators) enabling data-driven resource allocation for handling customer queries, ultimately enhancing support team efficiency. Show less
...
I am writing to highly recommend Furkhan Suhail for any data science-related opportunity. I had the pleasure of being Furkhan’s manager for more than 2 years as a Data Scientist at Microchip Technology Inc, where he consistently demonstrated outstanding technical expertise and dedication to his work. As a Data Scientist, Furkhan developed machine learning models that were integral to our business operations. He is highly skilled in a variety of machine learning techniques, including neural networks, linear and logistic regression, decision trees, and more. His expertise in using TensorFlow to develop and optimize these models was instrumental in driving significant improvements in the accuracy and efficiency of our predictive analytics.

Simplifying software development by leveraging artificial intelligence (AI) technology, Microchip Technology Inc. ’s iMPLAB AI Coding Assistant is set to have a major impact. A Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extension, this multi-faceted tool is totally free of charge. It will support engineers in their efforts when writing and debugging code - in order for the best possible results to thus be obtained. Based on the Continue open-source AI code assistant, it comes pre-configured with Microchip’s AI chatbot for real-time interaction. It allows engineers to evaluate and iterate code directly from the sidebar. The overall coding experience is enhanced, thanks to the highly customised and relevant real-time assistance provided. There is an advanced autocomplete function that facilitates coding. Alongside this, there are editing and error detection features that enable efficient code modifications within the current file and integrated access to searchable Microchip documentation within the integrated development environment (IDE).

# Microchip Hiring 2026 for Design Engineer Role | Freshers Can Apply
...
Microchip is hiring Design Engineers in their offices located in Chennai, Bengaluru and Hyderabad. This is a calling for candidates who possess a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree, thus making it an ideal opportunity for freshers and experienced professionals to create a career in the semiconductor and technology industry. Selected candidates will get an opportunity to work on innovative projects, gain hands-on experience as well as develop in a collaborative work environment. This recruitment drive is a great chance to join a renowned company in the world and grow professionally if you have a knack for engineering, design and cutting-edge technology.
...

...
- Design and Verification of IPs and Touch Controller products.

- Comply with and improve development methodology and quality flow.

...

...
- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering or related field.
- 0-3 years of experience.
- Experienced in digital or mixed signal design with a strong understanding.
- Experience in System Verilog for design and verification.
- Experienced with EDA design flow and tools
- Excellent problem-solving skills
- Individual contributor as well as a team player.

### About Microchip Company
...
Microchip Technology is an industry leader in the development, production and distribution of smart, connected and secure embedded control solutions. The Chandler, AZ-based firm was established in 1989 and offers products to customers in many different industries, including automotive, aerospace, industrial automation, consumer electronics, communications, and healthcare. It offers a wide range of products that covers the microcontroller, analog and mixed-signal, memory products, FPGAs, connectivity, and security technologies. Microchip’s commitment to innovation, reliability, and product support over the long term, enables engineers and developers to design efficient, high-value electronic systems for various applications across the globe.
...

### Where will the Microchip Design Engineer job be located?
...
The job location depends on business hiring and may include Microchip’s offices in cities such as Bangalore, Hyderabad, or Chennai, as mentioned in the official job posting.
...
### Can freshers apply for the Microchip Design Engineer job?
...
Yes, freshers who meet the educational qualifications and required technical skills are encouraged to apply for the Microchip Design Engineer position.
...
### Who is eligible for the Microchip Design Engineer role?
...
Candidates with a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electronics, Electrical Engineering, Electronics & Communication, Computer Engineering, or related disciplines are generally eligible. The exact eligibility criteria may vary based on the official job notification.

### https://www.linkedin.com/posts/madhu-hegde-baa98a7_workflow-automation-engines-and-agentic-ai-activity-7413879478428504064-My6d
...
Activity: Ganesh Krishna S M liked this
...
After 18+ years building firmware and embedded software, I've watched CI/CD evolve from Jenkins scripts to something far more interesting in the AI era: deterministic workflows orchestrating adaptive AI agents.
...
This is my article on the architectural divide between:
...
·       Graph-based workflow automation (visual, composable, deterministic)
·       Agentic AI systems (LLM-powered reasoning with RAG + MCP tool servers)
...
And more importantly—how to integrate them without sacrificing the deterministic nature that embedded systems demand.
...
What's inside:
...
1.     Why Jenkins users should care about visual workflow tools like n8n
2.     How AI agents plug in "smart steps" for log analysis and failure diagnosis
3.     MCP (Model Context Protocol) architecture for controlled tool access
4.     RAG systems querying hardware specs, errata sheets, and design docs
5.     The "workflows as spine, agents as muscles" integration pattern
...
For embedded engineers navigating the AI era, this is about rethinking development workflows with AI which adds adaptive intelligence at various stages of the process.
...
Read the full architectural breakdown #EmbeddedSystems #FirmwareDevelopment #AgenticAI #CICD #WiFi #MLOps #EmbeddedML

In various examples, the controller 106 may receive a write request from the host system 102. The write request may include user data to be written to one or more of the NVM media 116 of the memory device 114. The user data may include, for example, media (e.g., photos, videos, and/or audio), system information data, application data, sensor data, document data, recordkeeping data, machine learning/artificial intelligence data, gaming system data, data pertaining to internal operations of the host system, and the like, without limitation.
...
In various examples, a plurality of ML models may be stored on the local memory 110 and/or the ML component 112. The ML models may be trained during a manufacturing stage, such that the ML component 112 may utilize any of the ML models during operation. The ML models may be trained by another computing device, such as a computing system 200 of the manufacturer. Each of the ML models may include one or more of a regression model, a decision tree model, a support vector machine (SVM) model, a clustering model, an artificial neural network (ANN), a convolutional neural network (CNN), a recurrent neural network (RNN), a generative adversarial network (GAN), and/or the like, without limitation.
...
The ML models may be subject to supervised and/or unsupervised training. Training data may be generated based on error profiles of the WLs of the memory device 114 or of a similarly constructed memory device observed for generation of the training data. The training data may include labeled and/or unlabeled training data. The ML models may be trained on the training data to determine a read voltage threshold. Specific operations for training example ML models are described below with reference to FIGS. 7 and 8.

At operation 706, a ML model may be trained with the error profiles of the subset to determine a read voltage threshold. Training data may be generated from the error profiles of the subset. The training data may be provided as input to the ML model. The ML model may be trained to determine read voltage thresholds based on the training data. In various examples, the ML model may be iteratively trained until an accuracy criteria is met. An example training process is described in more detail in connection with the description of the method 800 of FIG. 8.

highlights:
On-site in Boise, Idaho, this mid-level role at Micron Technology involves developing AI-powered applications. The company leads in memory and storage solutions, focusing on transforming information into intelligence.
...
This role is ideal for developers who leverage AI tools like custom agents and Model Context Protocol to build scalable systems. It emphasizes AI in both development workflows and production systems, requiring skills in vibe coding and prompt engineering.
...
You'll design and integrate AI-driven solutions, focusing on automating routine tasks and embedding intelligence into workflows. Your work includes building custom AI agents, managing system architectures, and ensuring reliable, production-grade software.
...
We are seeking an experienced AI Full Stack Engineer to build modern applications where AI is a first-class capability—both in the development workflow and in production systems. This role focuses on designing and integrating AI-powered solutions using custom agents, Model Context Protocol (MCP), and scalable software engineering practices to accelerate development and deliver reliable, production-grade systems. Our team needs additional support to scale with the increased SMAI Data Science footprint and expand software support for Technology Development workflows and AI enablement.
...
We are looking for an AI Full Stack Engineer who builds modern applications using AI as a first-class capability—both as part of the development workflow and the systems we ship. This role goes beyond simply using AI coding assistants. It requires strong understanding of advanced timely creation, vibe scripting, Rework Rate Reduction, and applying Custom Agents with integrations built around MCP or equivalent experience. The goal is to significantly shorten development and feedback cycles by automating routine engineering work, augmenting human decision-making, and embedding intelligence into development workflows and delivered applications.
...
Build and implement Custom AI Agents to support workflows like task automation, decision support, summarization, multi-step reasoning, and guided execution. Show proficiency in timely engineering and Vibe Coding prototypes.

...
Integrate applications with external and internal AI services (LLMs, embeddings, search, tools) via well‑designed APIs, and harden AI components for 24×7 critical physical manufacturing systems with attention to reliability, resiliency, latency, performance, security, permissions, data boundaries, observability, and maintainability while applying retrieval‑augmented generation (RAG) systems
...
Use AI‑assisted tools to accelerate development (code generation, refactoring, test creation, documentation), critically evaluate and refine AI‑generated output, and apply human judgment to architecture, correctness, and long‑term maintainability
...
Experience designing and implementing Custom AI agents beyond simple chat interfaces, including developing skills for custom agents
...
AI‑native skills such as Prompt Engineering, Vibe Coding, context window management, and chain‑of‑thought design
...
Experience integrating systems using MCP or equivalent structured agent/tool interfaces and strong understanding of prompt design and tool invocation
...
, and hands‑on experience integrating RAG‑based systems
...
Experience applying AI‑enabled development practices to accelerate engineering workflows and improve productivity
...
Demonstrated ability to critically evaluate AI‑generated outputs and refine them for production‑grade systems
...

Role OverviewThe Embedded Gen AI Developer is a mid-senior software engineer who works directly inside of engineering team. The primary focus is hands-on delivery: building, integrating, and validating AI-assisted development capabilities within existing workflows. This is not a standalone AI project role— the developer becomes part of the client team, contributing within their processes, tools, and quality standards.Solid software engineering background with strong fundamentals in at least one primarylanguage used in the target environment• Practical Gen AI integration experience: using Azure OpenAI, GitHub Copilot, or equivalent toolsin a real delivery context• Understanding of agentic concepts: tool calling, prompt construction, context management,structured output handling• Familiarity with CI/CD practices and version control workflows (GitHub)• Ability to work independently within a client team with minimal hand-holdingPreferred Background• Experience integrating AI into software development workflows rather than building standaloneAI products• Exposure to Jira or Jama in a requirements or test management context, ideally in anASPICE-governed environment• Basic awareness of ISO 26262 or functional safety concepts — understanding that AI-generatedcode may feed into safety-relevant systems with traceability obligations• Familiarity with automotive or safety-critical software development environments• Experience with MCP or equivalent tool-use/function-calling integration patterns
...
#### Job responsibilities
...
• Develop and integrate Gen AI tooling within the client’s existing development environment andV-cycle workflows• Build and maintain coding agent pipelines using GitHub Copilot and Claude Code• Implement and extend MCP server integrations connecting AI agents to Jira, Jama, GitHub, andother internal systems• Contribute to CI/CD pipelines that incorporate AI-assisted code generation and validation steps• Work alongside client engineers as a peer contributor — not as an external consultant• Support verification and validation activities where AI-generated outputs require human reviewand structured testing

Today we continue to collaborate with clients in transforming businesses and redefining industries through intelligent products, platforms, and services.

results.title: AI-Agentic Analog IC Design – Career Center | Calvin University
highlights:
AI-Agentic Analog IC Design – Career Center | Calvin University
...
# AI-Agentic Analog IC Design
...
Fall Research Fellow: AI-Agentic Analog IC Design (Aug-Dec)
...
Location: Plano TX or Hybrid Position Type: Fall Research Fellowship (Eligible for Academic Credit Reimbursement)
...
The traditional analog IC design flow is highly manual, relying on expert intuition for topology selection, sizing, and iterative simulation. We are building the tool that changes this.
...
We are developing an AI-assisted design tool that leverages Large Language Models and agentic workflows to automate the generation, optimization, and verification of analog circuits. As a Summer Research Fellow, you won’t be fetching coffee or fixing minor UI bugs. You will be directly engineering the core AI-to-simulator pipeline, capturing expert engineering logic, and applying it to automated workflows.
...
Agentic Workflows: Develop and refine autonomous AI loops using frameworks like Antigravity or OpenClaw to translate high-level circuit requirements into executable design steps.
...
Simulator Integration: Build robust pipelines connecting LLMs with industry-standard simulators (like Spectre or Virtuoso) and PDKs to create a continuous feedback loop of generation, simulation, and optimization.
...
Intelligent RAG Systems: Assist in architecting local Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems to feed the LLM proprietary design knowledge and documentation.
...
You won’t be writing code in a vacuum. You will report directly to a seasoned engineering professional with deep expertise in analog IC design and AI-agent architectures. Expect a true apprenticeship model: you will receive weekly 1-on-1 architectural whiteboard sessions, rigorous code reviews, and direct guidance on how to navigate the intersection of hardware and machine learning. You will learn firsthand how a senior engineer breaks down complex, deterministic hardware problems and translates them into scalable, automated LLM pipelines.
...
Bare-Metal AI Compute: Direct access to an NVIDIA DGX Spark (128GB Grace Blackwell architecture) for running massive local models without latency or memory constraints.
...
Very Generous API Access: A massive, near unconstrained token budget for experimenting with frontier cloud models—if a workflow requires heavy API usage to test a theory, you will have the green light.
...
The Ultimate Resume Builder: The intersection of AI and Hardware/Silicon is currently the most heavily funded and sought-after niche in tech. You will leave with tangible proof that you can build complex, agentic AI tools for deeply technical domains.
...
We are a small, highly agile startup team operating at
...
bleeding edge of AI hardware application. There is zero corporate bureaucracy here—you won’t be a small cog in a massive machine.
...
An Engineering Student (MS, or PhD): Currently studying Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or a closely related field.
...
A Coder: Strong proficiency in Python and an eagerness to dive into the latest AI frameworks.
...
A Hardware Enthusiast: A foundational understanding of analog circuit design (OTAs, Current Mirrors, Biasing circuit, oscillators, comparators, PLLs) and layout.
...
A Hacker: Someone who is comfortable navigating new APIs, troubleshooting complex logic, and pushing the boundaries of what LLMs can do in a strictly deterministic engineering environment.
...
Send over your resume and a brief note about a project you’ve built (hardware, software, or AI) that you are particularly proud of.

results.title: Mikhail Chukhlebov
highlights:
Software Architect, Project Lead, System Developer. In the areas of Artificial Intelligence, Distributed Computational Systems and FPGA Programming aspects. Have many years of experience in software and semiconductors.
...
Software architect, AI platforms architect, business owner with many years of software development experience in semiconductor and AI industry: Microsemi SOC Projects and Automated Test Equipment (ATE) at Advantest America R&D Center. 7+ years in other industries. Experience of building and managing software project teams.
...
I am managing group of 12 people located in distributed environment. I am responsible for architecture and execution of the projects. Activities involve software and hardware solutions. It is also security intensive assuming programming of our FPGAs in untrusted environments (using HSM modules). I am involved in three big projects: remote programming/debug solution, next generation of our FPGA programmer and mass production solution. I am also responsible for creating initial prototypes for my architecture proposals and then driving teams on their development. Most of work involves C++ system level applications, interprocess communications, etc. I am responsible for adopting new technologies by my teams to achieve project goals. My other responsibility is customer support. It involves initial customer evaluation of our secure programming solution (SPPS), all kinds of its security certification, including Government level. Support of customer mass production process with the help of HSM servers. Lastly, I am involved in Artificial Intelligence development work mostly based on Intel OpenVino platform for running inference on various model topologies. I am also using Nvidia Digits platform over WSL2 for model training tasks.

# Data Scientist (100%)
...
## Drive innovation in machine learning and AI, from research to deployment, creating scalable intelligent systems that power the next generation of scientific discovery.
...
Are you passionate about turning complex data into intelligent, production-ready solutions while supporting the future of open-access science? We are looking for a Data Scientist to join our team and help design, develop, and deploy advanced machine learning solutions that power intelligent, data-driven products. This role focuses on NLP, recommender systems, and agentic LLM workflows, turning complex business challenges into scalable and impactful analytical solutions.
...
This is an opportunity to work at the forefront of machine learning and AI, contributing to innovative systems that leverage state-of-the-art research in practical applications.
...
Core Responsibilities
...
Design, develop, and evaluate machine learning and statistical models, with a focus on NLP, recommender systems, and LLM-based solutions.
...
Translate business problems into data-driven approaches and scalable analytical solutions.
...
Perform data exploration, preprocessing, and feature engineering to ensure high-quality inputs for modeling.
...
Develop, optimize, and benchmark models using appropriate metrics and validation strategies.
...
Build and experiment with LLM-powered systems, including agentic workflows and RAG.
...
Investigate, reproduce, and compare research prototypes and state-of-the-art methods.
...
Collaborate with engineering teams to support the integration of models into production.
...
Monitor and analyze model performance and iterate to improve accuracy and robustness.
...
Bachelor’s degree/ Master's degree in Computer Science or related.
...
2-5 years of experience as a Data Scientist.
...
2–5 years of experience in Python (including complex applications), Machine Learning, and LLMs.
...
Strong background in data science, statistics, and analytical problem-solving.
...
Intermediate proficiency in FastAPI, Celery, and Keycloak.
...
Intermediate proficiency in PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, and Hugging Face.
...
Proficiency in Natural Language Processing (NLP), including tokenization and named entity recognition (NER).
...
In-depth understanding of Artificial Intelligence principles.
...
Strong working knowledge of Microsoft O365 tools.
